BREAKING: Ghana's SEC just approved 11 companies into its crypto regulatory sandbox under the Virtual Asset Service Providers Act.

This signals Africa's largest economies are moving from prohibition to structured innovation—watch for other nations to follow.

entries.title: Kraken and Nasdaq Partnership Signals Shift to On-Chain Capital Markets
entries.snippet: Kava (@KAVA_CHAIN): This @krakenfx - @Nasdaq partnership is the signal. Tokenized equities trading around the clock on crypto infrastructure with Fed settlement access? The dam is breaking. On-chain capital markets are transitioning from 'when' to 'now.'

entries.title: Metaplanet Deepens Bitcoin Strategy With $25M Investment Plan, New Venture Arm
entries.snippet: Metaplanet is launching two subsidiaries and backing Japan's first licensed yen stablecoin as it bets on the country's digital asset infrastructure.

entries.title: Metaplanet launches VC and asset management subsidiaries, bets on Japanese stablecoin JPYC
entries.snippet: The venture capital arm will deploy 4 billion yen ($25 million) over the next few years to local bitcoin infrastructure firms.
entries.tweet_text:
Metaplanet is betting $25M on Japanese crypto infrastructure through new VC and asset management arms, backing the JPYC stablecoin ecosystem.

Watch for institutional adoption signals as Japan positions itself as a stablecoin hub competing with Singapore and Dubai.

entries.title: Binance.US names new CEO as crypto exchange eyes expansion
entries.snippet: Stephen Gregory, a former compliance executive at CEX.IO and Gemini, has taken over as CEO of Binance.US, a crypto exchange that was once a target of a long-running SEC lawsuit.

entries.title: Ghana greenlights 11 crypto companies for regulatory sandbox
entries.snippet: Ghana’s SEC could grant a full license to sandbox participants as early as six months, provided that their products are market-ready and that they tick all regulatory boxes.

entries.title: Binance sues the Wall Street Journal over DOJ investigation claims, Mastercard launches a crypto partner program with Ripple and PayPal, and February CPI comes in at 0.3%

entries.title: FDIC Plans to Block Stablecoins From Pass-Through Insurance
entries.snippet: The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) plans to propose rules saying that stablecoins are not eligible for FDIC pass-through insurance, and that tokenized deposits are eligible for such insurance. FDIC Chairman Travis Hill said this on Wednesday (March 11) in a speech delivered at the Amer
entries.tweet_text:
Will the FDIC's push to recognize tokenized deposits while blocking stablecoin insurance reshape digital asset regulation?

Watch for implementation timelines—this could accelerate institutional adoption of on-chain banking infrastructure.

entries.title: FDIC chair says no deposit insurance for stablecoins under GENIUS Act
entries.snippet: A proposed plan by the agency would ban “pass-through insurance“ for stablecoins by third parties in addition to the FDIC not insuring deposits under the law.
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: FDIC chair moves to block deposit insurance for stablecoins under proposed GENIUS Act, including third-party pass-through coverage.

Stablecoin issuers lose a key competitive advantage against traditional banking.

entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: FDIC won't extend deposit insurance to stablecoins under new GENIUS framework, shutting a major door for mainstream adoption.

This signals regulators see stablecoins as fundamentally different from bank deposits—a critical setback for the sector's TradFi integration.

entries.title: Mastercard Teams With Crypto Giants on Blockchain Payment Program
entries.snippet: Mastercard has launched an initiative aimed at exploring the future of cryptocurrency payments. The company’s Crypto Partner Program, announced Wednesday (March 11), brings together 85 different digital asset and payments companies, including high-profile players like Binance, Circle, Gemini, PayPal
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: @Mastercard convenes 85 crypto firms including Binance, Circle, and PayPal for blockchain payment exploration.

What traditionalists need to know: this signals institutional validation of stablecoin infrastructure moving mainstream.

entries.title: European Central Bank unveils tokenized finance plan to bolster EU's financial autonomy

This move could reshape how Europe competes globally in digital assets—watch for regulatory frameworks that follow.

entries.title: Ontop taps BVNK to add stablecoins to payrolls
entries.snippet: Ontop, the global workforce payments and payroll platform, today announced a strategic partnership with stablecoin infrastructure provider BVNK to launch embedded USD stablecoin accounts for international customers.
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: Stablecoins are now moving into payroll infrastructure. @Ontop partners with @BVNK to embed USD stablecoin accounts directly into international worker payments.

This signals crypto's shift from speculation to essential financial plumbing for global labor.

entries.title: BitGo to custody digital assets for StableX's $100M stablecoin plan

Enterprise-grade custody signals stablecoin ecosystems are entering regulated, institutional-grade operations.

entries.title: Societe Generale-FORGE launches EURCV stablecoin on Stellar

entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: @SocieteGenerale's FORGE expands euro stablecoin to Stellar, bringing MiCA-compliant digital assets to a second blockchain.

Regulated stablecoins on multiple chains signal traditional finance's commitment to decentralized infrastructure at scale.

entries.title: Can Crypto's Open Network Dreams Survive Going Corporate?
entries.snippet: The reality of financial blockchain adoption is turning out to be a very different story than the permissionless approach the digital asset space was built atop philosophically. Institutions including JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Citi, HSBC and BNY Mellon have all launched blockchain-based platfor
entries.tweet_text:
Crypto's founding promise of permissionless finance is colliding with reality as @JPMorgan, @GoldmanSachs, and other giants build proprietary blockchains.

The question: Can decentralization survive institutionalization?
